calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-20-2019 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-04-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendants Daniel Patterson And Mary Pattersons Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kelley Gelini, Esq. 415-891-8106 of Wakeford Gelini for Plaintiff.
David Hayek, Esq. 626-373-2444 of P.K. Schrieffer LLP for Defendants DANIEL PATTERSON, MARY PATTERSON.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant clarifies the tentative ruling and argues duty to remedy. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DANIEL PATTERSON, MARY PATTERSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant Pattersons' motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. Defendants fail to maintain their initial burden of production. Defendants fail to present evidence showing that the stairs and/or handrail did not constitute a dangerous condition of property. Even if the danger was "open and obvious," such a circumstance did not relieve defendants of their duty to remedy the condition. (See Osborn v. Mission Ready Mix (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 104, 119; Martinez v. Chippewa Enterprises, Inc. (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 1179, 1184.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
